issue_body:
Hi all,

First of all, many thanks for the good work!

I am experiencing an issue when trying to plot a CircleSkyRegion at a map edge in Aitoff projection. I followed the issue [#76](https://github.com/astropy/regions/issues/76) to properly project a circle on the sky in this projection.

My test displays a polygon which is filled across the whole longitude band, but empty where it is expected to be filled. I attach here a plot illustrating this, as well as a minimal working example:
![mwe](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5399958/30940785-a52054ee-a3e2-11e7-9bc2-0d9f76cef9ec.png)
```
#!/bin/env python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import astropy.coordinates as c
import astropy.units as u
from gammapy.maps import WcsMapND
from regions import CircleSkyRegion

fig = plt.figure()
m = WcsMapND.create(binsz=1.0, width=(360., 180.), skydir=(0.0, 0.0),
                    coordsys='GAL', proj='AIT')
m.fill_poisson(1.0)
wcs = m.geom.wcs
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection=wcs)
ax.imshow(m.data, cmap='plasma', alpha=0.2)

center = c.SkyCoord(l=180.2*u.degree, b=61.*u.degree, frame='galactic')
rad = 20.0*u.degree
region = CircleSkyRegion(center=center, radius=c.Angle(rad))
region.to_pixel(wcs=wcs, mode='full').plot(ax, color='green')

plt.show()
```
Is there a known workaround ?

Many thanks in advance.

issue_title: Should kwarg unit override header BUNIT
issue_body:
I got a bug report saying that ``aperture_photometry`` fails as the current code requires the ``unit`` kwarg to be the same unit as the header ``BUNIT``. However ``BUNIT`` is ``'JY/BEAM'`` in this case which is unrecognisable by the parser, and thus ``unit`` cannot override it with the correct format.

I cannot find any notes why we chose to have this requirement and not the other way, but propose the following two options to solve this:
  1) Have ``unit`` override ``BUNIT`` (and maybe other ``dataunit``, too)
  2) Check if ``BUNIT`` is Unrecognizable, and if yes allow ``unit`` to override.

I prefer the second option, but if your experience says that ``BUNIT`` tend to be wrong in some cases, then I see the logic doing the first option. PR to follow when this is decided.

cc: @astrofrog, @larrybradley

issue_title: Can matplotlib be made an optional dependency?
issue_body: I am using only a small part of scikit-image in a non-interactive environment, currently the skimage.filter.rank functionality and it is a bit suboptimal that my package then indirectly has to depend on the quite big matplotlib dependency, especially since I don't use it. For compiling matplotlib on linux you also have to install some system level libraries like libpng and freetype which may not exist in some headless servers by default. So, at least for me it would be a lot better if I could use scikit-image without matplotlib. It would be interesting to know how many parts of scikit-image depend on matplotlib.

issue_title: High memory usage during DAISY computation for large images
issue_body:
First of all I would like to thank all contributors for this excellent package and the continuous extension of features, I have been using scikit-image in my research for several years (and citing the webpage/paper).

Recently I used the HOG and DAISY descriptors for image classification and noticed that DAISY computation requires a lot of memory when working with large images (6 - 9 MPixel) due to its (though elegant) use of temporary arrays for intermediate results and vectorized computation.

I attached the output of [memory_profiler](https://github.com/fabianp/memory_profiler) for an image with 9Mpixels, which used **13.5 GiB** during computation. As I run out of memory when using `orientations` > 18, I am looking for workarounds. These do not necessarily have to involve changes to the codebase, but maybe a reduced memory footprint would benefit others as well.

Simple optimizations:
* delete intermediate results once they are no longer used (I had good results with explicitly calling `gc.collect()` in my batch scripts with many large images)
* split the computation for large images and merge resulting descriptors

What do you suggest?
(ping @andersbll as he authored the original PR)

issue_title: setup.py presumptuous (and possibly wrong) about how it will be used
issue_body:
There are a number of assumptions made in https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/setup.py that may not necessarily be true, or at least may force users to use it in a very specific way.  Here are a few examples:
- [ ] There is a reference to `pip` at https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/setup.py#L35, but the user may not be using `pip`, nor should `pip` be a requirement.
- [ ] There are references to `egg-info`.  What about wheels?  What if I'm not using eggs?
- [ ] A symlink is created automatically by default, regardless of how `setup.py` is used (i.e. when `python setup.py --help` is called, which doesn't actually print a descriptive help screen, but instead proceeds to build)
- [ ] setup.py overrides `__file__` in a way that seems to break convention

cc @scottpurdy
We should use [redislite](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/redislite) as a backend, see https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/pull/127

issue_title: BibField: clean up `doi` field and other FIXME in `atlantis.cfg`
issue_body:
Similarly to #1557, there are more clean-ups to be done in `atlantis.cfg`.  For example, in `invenio.conf` we have:

```
CFG_OAI_ID_FIELD = 909COo
CFG_OAI_SET_FIELD = 909COp
CFG_OAI_PREVIOUS_SET_FIELD = 909COq
```

while in `atlantis.cfg` we have:

```
@persistent_identifier(4)
oai:
    creator:
        @legacy((("024", "0248_", "0248_%"), ""),
                ("0248_a", "oai"),
                ("0248_p", "indicator"))
        marc, "0248_", {'value': value['a'], 'indicator': value['p']}
    producer: 
        json_for_marc(), {"0248_a": "oai", "0248_p": "indicator"}
```

and:

```
FIXME_OAI:
    creator:
        @legacy((('909', '909CO', '909CO%'), ''),
                ('909COi', 'group'),
                ('909COs', 'number'),
                ('909COo', 'id'),
                ('909COp', 'set'),
                ('909COq', 'previous_set'))
        marc, "909CO", {'group':value['i'], 'number':value['s'], 'id':value['o'], 'set':value['p'], 'previous_set':value['q']}
```

Notice the naming differences: e.g. "oai.indicator" should rather be called "oai.set"; "oai.value" should rather be called "oai.id".

Historically, `909CO` MARC tag in Invenio Atlantis defaults was used to store OAI information.  Later, `0248` was chosen on CDS and elswhere as a better MARC tag location.  Invenio Atlantis defaults were not changed though, which permitted e.g. to test easily how Invenio is configurable for various site conditions.

However, as it is agreed that `0248` is better OAI location, we should probably move defaults there, so that new Invenio installations are using by default "good" MARC tags already.

(This is similar with 773 vs 909C4, etc, see #1557.)

P.S. See also other FIXMEs in `atlantis.cfg`.  It would be useful to go through the file, fix all FIXMEs, and fix `demobibdata.xml` and `democfgdata.sql` and `invenio.conf` accordingly.

issue_title: Create admin.py (much like DRF authtoken admin.py)
issue_body:
Many users would benefit greatly from being able to view tokens already made/create new tokens/delete tokens in the Django Admin Panel. Currently DRF has an included admin.py with the `rest_framework.authtoken` app [found here](https://github.com/encode/django-rest-framework/blob/master/rest_framework/authtoken/admin.py).

When the `rest_framework.authtoken` app is installed it still creates a standard DRF token and not one from DRF-jwt. Is there a DRF configuration in settings.py to ensure DRF makes all tokens with JWT? Or can we have a standard admin.py installed with the app with the required fields to show DRF-jwt tokens?

issue_title: How to change the expiration per token
issue_body:
I just installed jwt and it is going well so far.  I need some tokens to never expire (i.e. set the expire date 100 years from now) and I need some to expire after say 2 hours.
How can I put an expiration on a per token basis?

I imagine override the encode_handler?

Does anyone have a working example of dynamically changing the expiration?

Thank you!

issue_title: Can't import jnius.so dlopen failed file size=0
issue_body:
I'm trying to use jnius with crystax 2.7 python on an Android emulator (x86). Python runs fine and the app is able to import my other compiled c++ python modules (built with p4a) but importing jnius fails due to the following error:

```
    import jnius
File "/data/user/0/com.jventura.pyapp/assets/python/jnius/__init__.py", line 12 in <module>
    from .jnius import * #noqa
ImportError: dlopen failed: file offset for the library "/data/user/0/com.jventura.pyapp/assets/python/jnius/jnius.so" >= file size: 0>=0
```

Any idea why the so file is 0 bytes? The jnius.o file is 2.6 MB. Thanks!

I can't find a way to make buildozer working with syslink to include files and folder from another physical location rather than the root path of the main.py

issue_title: Propose more robust datetime to unix time conversion in yahoo/daily.py
issue_body:
Apologies as I'm new to git and github. I can try to make a fork/patch/pull request but I have to figure out how. In the meantime, I thought I'd offer the following to the local experts who surely can do this an order of magnitude faster than I can...

```
    def _get_params(self, symbol):
# I propose changing
        unix_start = int(time.mktime(self.start.timetuple()))
# to
        unix_start = (start - datetime.datetime(1970, 1, 1)).total_seconds()
# as it avoids mktime overflows
# e.g., try running the code with 1900/01/01 as the start range
```

Hello,

I think that all DataReaders are now objects except `wb.py` maybe this file need a rewrite.

see also https://github.com/pydata/pandas-datareader/issues/117

Kind regards

I was running fuzz testing on [my Brotli implementation](https://github.com/dsnet/compress/tree/master/brotli) and noticed that the following input differs when decompressed by my version compared to the canonical one here.

The input causing issues (in hex): `e2e184886a563080e0391603e430f9`

When decompressed by the `google/brotli` C implementation: 
`worldwhitemediafirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirst...`
When decompressed by my implementation: 
`worldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorl...`

According to section 4 of the RFC:
>   When a distance symbol 0 appears, the distance it represents (i.e.,
   the last distance in the sequence of distances) is not pushed to the
   ring buffer of last distances

However, this seems contrary to [what C decoder currently does](https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/ccabf811ffcef087d9f10cf417b05271c2067a82/dec/decode.c#L1706-L1710):
```c
if (s->distance_code >= 0) {
	--s->dist_rb_idx;
	s->distance_code = s->dist_rb[s->dist_rb_idx & 3];
	goto postReadDistance;  /* We already have the implicit distance */
}
```

The logic in the C version seems contrary to the stated prose in the RFC, which makes no mention that an implicit zero *reverse* rotates the distance ring-buffer.

issue_title: Have a calculateHalfLightRadius available for all GSObjects
issue_body:
Currently most of our simple GSObjects have a `getHalfLightRadius`, but not our more complicated ones.  The rationale for this is that it is not even well-defined what the half-light radius means for something that isn't radially symmetric.  And even if it were well-defined, it would require some work to calculate it for many of our objects.

However, this restricts our ability to set the size of an object according to a resolution parameter.  There is currently no easy way to set the size of a RealGalaxy (for example) according to a desired resolution.

I think a reasonable solution is to add a new function `calculateHalfLightRadius` that will check for a `getHalfLightRadius` function and return that if it's available.  But if not, then make an image and calculate the value using some reasonable definition of half-light radius.

Then we could draw objects with a specific resolution by constructing them with an arbitrary size value, calculating the half-light radius, and then dilating the profile appropriately.

Furthermore, half-light radius was a fairly arbitrary choice for how we defined the resolution.  Another reasonable option is Irr = Ixx + Iyy (or its square root).  This would be even easier to implement, since I think we can have a `calculateIrr` function that would just use the existing HSM code when there isn't an analytic option.  I don't know if there are other common definitions of resolution that we might want to enable, but these two would be a good start I think.

NeuroM neurites rely on a very generic tree representation. This had great advantages (for example, using the same underlying code to represent section trees instead of point trees.) But it also makes reasoning about some algorithms complicated by requiring a conceptual separation between the tree structure and the contents. For example, some functions need to know the tree structure, others only care about the section's points. I think now that we have converged on a suitable representation for neurites (trees of sections) it may be possible to lose some of the generality by changing the concept of "section" from a collection of points, to a tree node holding a collection of points. So a "section" could be represented similarly to out `Tree` class. In json-style pseudocode:

```
section = { 
    parent,
    points,
    children
}
```

By making this representation clear, developers would know that a "section function" assumes that data representation, i.e. it has access to the collection of points via the `points` member, and the tree-like components via `parent` and `children`. This will remove the conceptual difference between "tree functions" (e.g. branch_order, path_length) and pure "section functions (i.e. functions that only care about the points in the section.) I think the loss of generality is worth it.

My proposal would be to keep a generic tree base class, and derive `Section` from it:

```
class BaseTree(object):
    def __init__(self):
        self.parent = None
        self.children = list()
    def add_child(self, child):
        child.parent = self
        self.children.append(child)
        return child
```

issue_title: Heads-up: Etcd3 will require significant changes
issue_body:
Etcd3 will require significant changes for compatibility:
1. protobufs and base-64 encoded keys mean that we can't use the same keying;
2. elimination of tree structures for a flat keyspace means that we have to change how we name the keys

Possibly python drivers will handle this for us, but maybe not.

https://coreos.com/blog/etcd3-a-new-etcd.html

Currently, `patronictl switchover` will list a `nofailover` host as an option to switch to, but then fail if you try to do it saying that there are no candidates.

I think `nofailover` nodes should either be hidden from the candidate list during switchover/failover or let you fail over to them (perhaps with a warning/confirmation). Doesn't make sense to let the user choose them and then fail because they're tagged as `nofailover`.

issue_title: Exporter loads the html5lib engine even if it isn't used
issue_body:
At the moment, the exporter loads the html5lib engine even if it is configured to use another one. The impact is 11.6MB of memory taken for nothing:

```text
Line #    Mem usage    Increment   Line Contents
================================================
     8     11.8 MiB      0.0 MiB   @profile
     9                             def run():
    10     23.4 MiB     11.6 MiB       from draftjs_exporter.html import HTML
    11
    12     23.4 MiB      0.0 MiB       HTML({'engine': 'string'})
[...]
```

Small demo script:

<details>

```py
from memory_profiler import profile


@profile
def run():
    from draftjs_exporter.html import HTML

    HTML({'engine': 'string'}).render({
        'entityMap': {},
        'blocks': [
            {
                "key": "2nols",
                "text": "Test",
                "type": "unstyled",
                "depth": 0,
                "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                "entityRanges": [],
                "data": {}
            },
        ]
    })


run()
```

</details>

This could be a relatively simple fix (see patch below) if `DOM.use` was used consistently, but there are many tests that aren't calling it explicitly (and thus implicitly rely on the html5lib default). I would suggest to wait for #79 to be taken care of, so we don't have to update all those tests and they can then rely on the new default.

```diff
--- a/draftjs_exporter/dom.py
+++ b/draftjs_exporter/dom.py
@@ -3,7 +3,6 @@ from __future__ import absolute_import, unicode_literals
 import inspect
 import re

-from draftjs_exporter.engines.html5lib import DOM_HTML5LIB
 from draftjs_exporter.error import ConfigException

 # Python 2/3 unicode compatibility hack.
@@ -28,7 +27,7 @@ class DOM(object):
     LXML = 'lxml'
     STRING = 'string'

-    dom = DOM_HTML5LIB
+    dom = HTML5LIB

     @staticmethod
     def camel_to_dash(camel_cased_str):
@@ -37,7 +36,7 @@ class DOM(object):
         return dashed_case_str.replace('--', '-')

     @classmethod
-    def use(cls, engine=DOM_HTML5LIB):
+    def use(cls, engine=HTML5LIB):
         """
         Choose which DOM implementation to use.
         """
@@ -45,6 +44,7 @@ class DOM(object):
             if inspect.isclass(engine):
                 cls.dom = engine
             elif engine.lower() == cls.HTML5LIB:
+                from draftjs_exporter.engines.html5lib import DOM_HTML5LIB
                 cls.dom = DOM_HTML5LIB
             elif engine.lower() == cls.LXML:
```

issue_title: Buggy $LINES and $COLUMNS not being set properly
issue_body:
When opening the first Guake tab, `$LINES` and `$COLUMNS` aren't set to reflect the size of the actual terminal, and are set as the default `24` and `80`, respectively.

I'm using Fish shell, and here is how to identify the issue. Add the following to the init file (`~/.config/fish/config.fish`):

```fish
echo "Size: $LINES x $COLUMNS"
```

Then opening the terminal gives the following output, even when the terminal is of a different size:

```fish
Size: 24 x 80
$> echo "Size: $LINES x $COLUMNS"
Size: 38 x 239
$> 
```

Observe that the variables are set correctly _after_ config files are loaded, which causes issues when running programs in init files that care about the terminal size.

Using a signal hook, I could observe the reason for this bug. Guake is sending `SIGWINCH` to the terminal, but after an unacceptable delay that causes issues with init scripts. `~/.config/fish/config.fish`:

```fish
echo "Size: $LINES x $COLUMNS"

function signal-hook -on-signal SIGWINCH
  echo "Size: $LINES x $COLUMNS"
end
```

And opening the terminal again:

```fish
Size: 24 x 80
$> Size: 38 x 239
$> 
```

The signal handler is triggered after some time (less than 1 second) showing the correct values.

Interestingly enough, none of these issues occur when creating a _new_ tab after this already happens to the first tab.

issue_title: Fix fuzzy legislation values in the past
issue_body:
In `param.xml` there are some `fuzzy` values. Some of them are in the past. They must be replaced by real values, in general coming from "IPP baremes".

> `fuzzy` values in the future may not by removed.

See https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/issues/368 for background information.

Les modalités de calcul de la PAJE pour les enfants nés après le 1er avril 2014 ont été changées.

Il faudra s'organiser pour corriger ça.

[Ceci est un reminder]

Comme indiqué dans https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/788#pullrequestreview-57221670 :

> `versement_transport.taux` n'est [pas chargé comme un paramètre](https://legislation.openfisca.fr/?q=versement_transport.taux#search-input) 😞
Il est [utilisé](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/master/openfisca_france/model/prelevements_obligatoires/prelevements_sociaux/contributions_sociales/versement_transport.py#L54-L69) comme une `global` 😱

Il n'y a donc aujourd'hui pas de moyen de découvrir ces valeurs, ni leur usage dans OpenFisca.

Je propose donc d'introduire a minima un paramètre vide dont la description explique comment accéder aux valeurs, et au mieux d'ajouter dynamiquement ces valeurs à la liste des paramètres afin de les exposer via l'API.

issue_labels: [{'id': 589015854, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw1ODkwMTU4NTQ=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/trailofbits/manticore/labels/easy', 'name': 'easy', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 725008567, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3MjUwMDg1Njc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/trailofbits/manticore/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': True}, {'id': 679198275, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2NzkxOTgyNzU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/trailofbits/manticore/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': 'f9d0c4', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
### OS / Environment
<!--- lsb_release -a --->

### Manticore version
<!--- pip show manticore | grep Version --->
8142472c50617c130151ce701bb67a7f98972708

### Python version
<!--- python --version --->


### Dependencies
<!--- pip freeze --->


### Summary of the problem

Currently Manticore.terminate() does not abandon the current state. All it does it set the shutdown flag within the executor, which causes no more states to be loaded, and for the executor to stop _at the next execute loop cycle_.

Typically Manticore.terminate() is called from within a hook.

```
@m.hook(0x41414141)
def hook(state):
  # stuff
  m.terminate()  # expects for manticore to completely exit, and for the m.run() function to return at this point
```

the `@m.hook()` syntactic sugar is just a `will_execute_instruction` event handler. that event is fired in `abstractcpu.py:Cpu.execute` before the instruction is executed. so if the hook calls `m.terminate()` and returns, manticore will actually not stop then. that instruction will continue to execute, and manticore will potentially do (lots?) of other stuff. manticore will only stop until the next iteration of the execute loop begins.

in order to actually implement the semantics of Manticore.terminate() we should probably raise `TerminateState()` within m.terminate() to force the state to halt. since this forces a new iteration of the execute loop, this will effectively stop the engine.


https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/8142472c50617c130151ce701bb67a7f98972708/manticore/manticore.py#L613-L618

todo:

- raise `TerminateState` at the end of `Manticore.terminate()`. maybe something like `TerminateState('Mantiocre terminated')`

issue_title: blocked reactions should report a warning
issue_body:
Right now blocked reactions are reported as an error. Maybe this should instead be a warning? Or not a warning at all ?

There could be blocked reactions due to problems with gap filling, but there might also be cases where a reaction is blocked and still biologically relevant (for instance by resulting from the side activity of a promiscuous reaction). 

These reactions should not be removed from the model, even if they cannot carry flux, as they still represent biological processes that can occur inside the cell if the right conditions are met (eg: being reconnected after insertion of a heterologous pathway during strain design).

}

How can specify a partitioning scheme as above:

``` python
    job_config = bigquery.QueryJobConfig()

    dest_table_name = 'YOUR_TABLE'

issue_body:
BigQuery [tabledata.insertAll](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/tabledata/insertAll) supports inserting rows with columns that take record types (nested objects).

A customer reports that

> My table structure has nested schemas. The [[google-cloud-python] docs](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/stable/bigquery-table.html#google.cloud.bigquery.table.Table.insert_data) don't say how to handle nested structures. All we get is, "rows (list of tuples) – Row data to be inserted. Each tuple should contain data for each schema field on the current table and in the same order as the schema fields." A list of tuples. That's it. If I have nested data do I make sub-tuples? Is it simply not supported at this time? 
> 
> I dug into the BQ code to find out what was going on. BQ does, 
> 
> zip(table._schema, rows) 
> 
> which looks like it won't support nested inserts (or could -- with sub-tuples, but who knows). 
> 

Example in the API explorer, with the following table schema:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/247555/22127258/1743ad4a-de50-11e6-9a71-94433ecded92.png)

Try it out in the [API Explorer for bigquery.tabledata.insertAll](https://developers.google.com/apis-explorer/#p/bigquery/v2/bigquery.tabledata.insertAll). Enter the following for the request body (after creating a table with the same schema). You need to switch the editor to free-form mode

```
{
  "rows": 
  [
    {
      "insert_id": "12345",
      "json": 
      {
        "string_col": "Some value",
        "record_col": {
          "nested_string": "another string value",
          "nested_repeated": ["0", "1", "2"],
          "nested_record": {
            "nested_nested_string": "some deep insight"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

I can verify that this works with a SQL query.

```
#standardSql
SELECT * FROM `swast-scratch.hello_world.nested` LIMIT 1000
```

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/247555/22130561/a792fdb8-de61-11e6-8f5a-f25f52dfdc31.png)

It is not clear from the docs how to do a similar call from our client libraries (and it likely isn't possible).

In order to navigate (potentially broad) directory hierarchies in buckets, it would be useful to be able to list the sub-directories in a bucket at a given prefix. Happy to attempt to implement such a method if someone points me in the correct direction. Seems like perhaps a new Iterator?

issue_title: Relation between account_daemon and oslogin tools
issue_body:
I am using [NixOS](https://nixos.org/) on GCE and am looking into integrating authorization/authentication features. I see a certain redundancy in the provided features and I would like to confirm that my conceptual understanding is correct. Could you please tell me if I understand it correctly that

1. `google_authorized_keys` executable, `pam_oslogin_{login,admin}.so` modules and `nss_oslogin` modules always query the metadata server and do not rely in any way on `google_account_daemon`.
2. the primary goal of `google_account_daemon` is not to keep a local copy of user/account/rights from the metadata server but to keep the system database of users (`/etc/passwd`) in sync.

I see that the provided set of tools provides a lot flexibility. What I do not quite understand is the motivation to duplicate/maintain GCE user accounts the standard UNIX user/group database. The alternative I have in mind is that GCE daemon
1. maintains GCE accounts in sync with the metadata server
2. exposes them via PAM modules which consumers are configured to use.

With this design GCE and system accounts never mix so that if something fails it is easier to identify what went wrong. The big difference is that deployment would require to reconfigure all the relevant PAM consumers to use GCE modules. I don't see it as disadvantage as the resulting permissions would arguably become much more transparent to the user.

issue_title: Table header border client style is not reset

issue_body:
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/blob/master/assets/stylesheets/bootstrap/_glyphicons.scss looks to still contains several now-questionable formats:

- there are no longer any browsers that support `eot`, as the only browsers that supported `eot` were Microsoft IE4 through IE8, all of which have been finally killed off in January of 2016, with Microsoft only officially supporting IE11 and Edge, and for very special enterprise customers, IE9 on Vista SP2. All of these browsers support `woff`, thus making `eot` obsolete and redundant.
- the `svg` font format was abandoned when it become more than apparent it that it was wildly unsuitable for encoding fonts, to the point where the official SVG specifically *removed* the chapter on font-related markup, marking it as out-of-spec markup. SVG-compliant browsers have since removed support for it (because it's no longer part of the spec) and the only browser that still officially supports `svg` fonts also supports `woff`, making `svg` also both obsolete and redundant.
- the `ttf` and `otf` formats, as full-table, uncompressed system fonts, are also not necessary: browsers that support these formats also support `woff`, and should always prefer to load the `woff` resource if one is available. The only exception could be said to be Android 2.x through 4.2.x, but these versions have terrible web font support even when using `ttf` and are on the same level of "you're on your own" when it comes to webfonts as we used to be when we tried to make modern CSS work on IE6, 7, and 8. `ttf` and `otf` also don't enjoy the benefit of compression that `woff` comes with.

So for modern bootstrap, really the only formats needed are `woff` and `woff2`, with the latter of course being preferred because it has better compression for faster delivery over the wire.

issue_body:
**声明：文章原创，可以全文转载，禁止转载修改，禁止用于脚本出售，禁止抄袭**
**声明：本文仅供测试，请依照相关法律合法使用**

* [本文链接-个人网站，建议收藏](https://zhangdanyang.com/#/Post/5a75116be9116c2be23e7b5d)

* [本文链接-CSDN](http://blog.csdn.net/boxuerixin/article/details/79295976)

* [本文链接-简书](https://www.jianshu.com/p/74083a8ea6a9)

> VPS QQ讨论群：633509738。
> 有需要直接远程协助的，安装好远程协助工具TeamViewer 后Q我：958823752。（全程协助收费 ¥25/次）

# 请先读我
## 实现原理
本地发起连接请求，由远程服务器响应后然后将我们需要的数据返回到本地。

[了解更多戳我](http://vc2tea.com/whats-shadowsocks/)

## 最低消费

2.5美元（经常售罄，请选择纽约），5美元。

## 网速自测

经过我个人测试后选择的Dallas节点，浏览youtube视频，网速能达到1MB/s
，玩美服lol的延迟是200ms-250ms，这个速度已经很不错了，具体分析参考[《国内连接美国VPN延迟(PING)多少算正常？》](http://www.vpnps.com/usa_vpn_ping.html)。

PS：浏览其他人的博客都推荐的是LosAngeles节点，具体的自己通过测试后拿主意吧，懒得测试就选洛杉矶节点吧。

了解更多，请戳[Vultr 节点哪个比较快？](https://www.v2ex.com/t/276585)

## 知识储备
1. 懂Linux最好，不懂就按照下面操作来吧
2. 肯折腾
3. 懂英语，不懂的话…搭建SS（Shadow Socks），诶？

## 具体步骤
### 购买服务器
1. 打开[链接1：我的夏季促销推广链接](http://www.vultr.com/?ref=7044457-3B)，无效的话尝试 [链接2：我的普通推广链接](http://www.vultr.com/?ref=7039524)。
这两个都指向官网，信不过我的自己去百度搜索vultr官网。

2. 注册账号并验证邮箱。

3. 测试速度或直接选择洛杉矶节点，[测试节点网速请戳我并拉到页面最下面](https://www.vultr.com/faq/)。如果感觉不满意，去试试其他的服务器提供商比如搬瓦工等等，个人感觉vultr还可以。

补充：近期东京结点基本都挂掉了，美国的也挂掉了很多，请尝试洛杉矶结点或达拉斯结点。

4. 充值，点击左侧的Billing，最低$10，建议选择支付宝支付，简单快捷，符合我国国情。

5. 搭建服务器，点击左侧的Servers，依次选择Server Location——你测试的最快的或者洛杉矶；Server Type——Cent OS7 x64（这个我可以提供技术支持，本文基于CentOS 7 x64，**脚本原创，脚本基于Cent OS7**）；Server Size——只是搭建ss，选价格最低的就够了($2.5/mon，多数情况下此套餐售罄，请选择$5/mon)；其他的选填。然后点击右下角的Deploy Now。稍等片刻，服务器就可以装好了。

6. 装好后，你可以看到如下界面：

![servers](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-1ac7f1e803a837ea.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

点击可以查看服务器的相关信息：

![server information](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-94fdc78226d6cf94.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

接下来操作需要的信息是IP Address（IP地址），Username（用户名）和Password（密码）。这个页面不要关，一会直接复制粘贴相关信息。

### 远程连接服务器

> 方法①：使用网页版的进行操作。点击刚才的网页的右上角的五个按钮最左边的View Console进行操作。

> 方法②：使用ssh工具进行操作。 Windows用户使用putty或Xshell进行操作。Mac用户请使用终端或iTerm2进行操作。

#### 以Xshell为例（Windows用户请看）

1. 下载安装Xshell。
2. 安装完成后新建会话（快捷键Alt+N）。依次填写图中信息。
名称可以是Vultr或者其他，协议选择SSH，主机填写之前的IP Address，端口号选择22。

![连接](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-f322045231665e92.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

点击左侧的用户身份验证，填写信息。方法选择Password，用户名为之前的Username（一般都是root），密码为之前的Password（这个建议直接复制粘贴过来，系统给的有点复杂）

![用户身份验证](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-7b552de82575fe61.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

填写完之后点击确定。然后点击连接。出现其他提示的话选择接受就可以了。这时你就可以看到一个命令控制台了。这时就算连接成功了。

#### 以iTerm2为例（Mac用户请看）

1. 打开终端。
2. 输入命令。
```
ssh root@45.32.195.77
```
如果有提示很长一大段文字，服务器连接指纹认证，如下

![指纹验证](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-046a13945b6f62a3.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

输入
```
yes
```
接着出现

![请输入密码](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-78d901f620f45405.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

将系统给的密码复制过来进行粘贴，粘贴操作是不允许看到密码的，粘贴完直接回车即可。

这之后就连接上了服务器。你将看到如下界面：

![登录成功](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-ac875c4388253892.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

## 脚本快速安装（方案一，推荐用户：全体成员尤其是小白）

### 脚本功能
* 自定义**端口号**和**密码**，加密方式采用**aes-256-cfb**(脚本中采用此方式)
* 全过程静默安装，不会打扰用户，你所要做的就是去听一首音乐或者去喝杯咖啡
* 一次只允许运行一个shadowsocks进程，脚本会自动检测原来已经运行的进程并杀死
* 安装防火墙并开放需要的端口，实测vultr服务器不安装防火墙无法进行连接

### 操作步骤

1. 下载脚本
```
wget http://zhangdanyang.com/ss.sh
```
![执行](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-0491a88d1baf8ecd.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)


2. 执行脚本
```
bash ss.sh
```
    # 设置端口号并回车，直接回车是设置为1225
    Please enter PORT(1225 default):
    # 设置密码并回车，直接回车是设置为123456
    Please enter PASSWORD(123456 default):

    # 等待一会……就完成了（初次执行约2-5min）

### 具体图示

![步骤，蓝框内的是手动输入的](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-e0b5b26371252815.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

### gif 演示
![详细过程](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-575fe20efcfe327e.gif?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip)

### 脚本源码

[源码地址-简书](https://www.jianshu.com/p/68528e29f130)

[源码下载](http://zhangdanyang.com/ss.sh)

## 独立动手搭建（方案二，推荐用户：极客，爱折腾的人）

### 搭建 Shadowsocks 服务

#### 安装组件

    yum install m2crypto python-setuptools
    easy_install pip
    pip install shadowsocks

#### 安装完成后配置服务器参数
    vi  /etc/shadowsocks.json

写入如下配置:

    {
        "server":"0.0.0.0",
        "server_port":443,
        "local_address": "127.0.0.1",
        "local_port":1080,
        "password":"123456",
        "timeout":300,
        "method":"aes-256-cfb",
        "fast_open": false
    }

多端口的如下：

    {
        "server":"0.0.0.0",
        "local_address": "127.0.0.1",
        "local_port":1080,
        "port_password": {
             "443": "443",
             "8888": "8888"
         },
        "timeout":300,
        "method":"aes-256-cfb",
        "fast_open": false
    }

其中server字段与local_address填写之前的IP Address。password是自己用于连接这个shadow socks的密码，自定义就好。
其他的不需要更改。

然后保存退出。

vi 的命令: 按 "i" 进入编辑模式，编辑后按 "esc" 退出编辑模式， 输入 ":wq" 保存退出vi。

### 配置防火墙

    # 安装防火墙
    yum install firewalld
    # 启动防火墙
    systemctl start firewalld

#### 开启防火墙相应的端口

    # 端口号是你自己设置的端口
    firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-port=443/tcp
    firewall-cmd --reload

### 启动 Shadowsocks 服务

	# 后台运行    
    ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json -d start

	# 调试时使用下面命令，实时查看日志
    ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json


	
## 连接
这样服务器就搭建好了。[全平台的连接方法戳我](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/wiki)。

### PC连接
下载Shadow Socks客户端。[下载地址](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-windows/releases)
选择适合的版本，下载并解压运行。

填写信息:服务器地址，端口号，密码，加密方式与代理端口默认即可

![SS信息填写](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-4d8d781517796bde.png?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

填写完之后点击确定，然后**到托盘中右键选择开启"启用系统代理"**。

### iOS连接

在App Store下载Wingy。

填写信息:服务器，端口，密码，代理模式，加密方式默认即可。

![Wingy信息填写](http://upload-images.jianshu.io/upload_images/606686-84ea88f5860d36cf.PNG?imageMogr2/auto-orient/strip%7CimageView2/2/w/1240)

### MacOS连接

[下载地址](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-iOS/releases)
使用方式参考windows

### Android连接

[下载地址](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/releases)

## 国外站点

[Google](http://www.google.com/)

[Youtube](http://www.youtube.com/)

[Facebook](http://www.facebook.com/)

如果以上没有问题的话，这时候你就可以畅游外面的世界了。点击上述链接测试吧。

## 常见问题

### 远程连接工具xshell无法连接服务器。

此时ping一下服务器，如果ping不同，则证明创建的服务器ip被墙，请销毁掉当前服务器，重新创建新的服务器。

### 计费模式

服务器按照小时计费，如果一台服务器创建1天后就销毁了，那么只扣1天的费用。例如：服务器一个月$5，那么1天扣除的就是5/30美元。


### 脚本源码


``` bash
#! /bin/bash
# log路径
export log_path=/etc/ss.log
# 设置端口号
echo -n -e '\033[36mPlease enter PORT(1225 default): \033[0m'
# echo -n "please enter port(1225 default):"
read port
if [ ! -n "$port" ];then
        echo "port will be set to 1225"
        port=1225
else
        echo "port will be set to $port"
fi
# 设置密码
echo -n -e '\033[36mPlease enter PASSWORD(123456 default): \033[0m'
# echo -n "please enter password(123456 default):"
read pwd
if [ ! -n "$pwd" ];then
        echo "password will be set to 123456"
        pwd=123456
else
        echo "password will be set to $pwd"
fi
# 写shadowsocks.json配置文件
cat>/etc/shadowsocks.json<<EOF
{
    "server":"0.0.0.0",
    "server_port":$port,
    "local_address": "127.0.0.1",
    "local_port":1080,
    "password":"$pwd",
    "timeout":300,
    "method":"aes-256-cfb",
    "fast_open": false
}
EOF
# 安装 shadowsocks 防火墙等
ret=`yum install -y m2crypto python-setuptools >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`easy_install pip >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`pip install shadowsocks >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`yum install -y firewalld >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`systemctl start firewalld >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
# 开启端口
ret=`firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-port=22/tcp >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-port=$port/tcp >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
ret=`firewall-cmd --reload >> ${log_path} 2>&1`
# 如果有相同功能的进程则杀死
ps -ef|grep ssserver|grep shadowsocks|grep -v grep  
if [ $? -eq 0 ];then  
    ps -ef|grep ssserver|grep shadowsocks|awk '{ print $2 }'|xargs kill -9 
fi
# 后台运行
/usr/bin/ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json -d start
# 成功
if [ $? -eq 0 ];then
clear
cat<<EOF
***************Congratulation!*************
Shadowsocks installed successfully!

PORT: $port
PASSWORD: $pwd
METHOD: aes-256-cfb

***************JUST ENJOY IT!**************
EOF
# 失败
else
clear
cat<<EOF
************Failed,retry please!***********

cat /etc/ss.log to get something you need…

************Failed,retry please!***********
EOF
fi
```

I think this were added fairly recently with tags support for AWS Lambda.

/cc @phobologic

issue_title: Have a non ivy way to show jar info in the 'export' format, just use real coordinates
issue_body:
The representation of jar deps in the export file is very confusing.  

The mapping for libraries can be either an ivy 'conf' or a 'classifler'.   FYI @gmalmquist @wisechengyi 

```
  @staticmethod
  def _jar_id(jar):
    """Create a string identifier for the IvyModuleRef key.
    :param IvyModuleRef jar: key for a resolved jar
    :returns: String representing the key as a maven coordinate
    """
    if jar.rev:
      return '{0}:{1}:{2}'.format(jar.org, jar.name, jar.rev)
    else:
      return '{0}:{1}'.format(jar.org, jar.name)

  def _resolve_jars_info(self, targets, classpath_products):
    """Consults ivy_jar_products to export the external libraries.

    :return: mapping of jar_id -> { 'default'     : <jar_file>,
                                    'sources'     : <jar_file>,
                                    'javadoc'     : <jar_file>,
                                    <other_confs> : <jar_file>,
                                  }
    """
    mapping = defaultdict(dict)
    jar_products = classpath_products.get_artifact_classpath_entries_for_targets(
      targets, respect_excludes=False)
    for conf, jar_entry in jar_products:
      conf = jar_entry.coordinate.classifier or 'default'
      mapping[self._jar_id(jar_entry.coordinate)][conf] = jar_entry.cache_path
    return mapping
```

Then elsewhere there is special logic to not include the classifier and type in the export.

1) We have to have special logic now to interpret classifiers.  Basically just ignore the string 'default' for the regular jar and everything else is a classifier.
2) This doesn't really fit the ivy conf model - confs can return multiple jars
3) We are losing the type field


I think we should:
1)  Dump the jar dependency information just as a straight M2 Coordinate. 
2) The jar_library target holds a list of jars. Just do that - no need to do this map indexed by classifiers.

 Tools will have to be smart enough to know that 'sources' and 'javadoc' classifiers are special jars used only for IDEs but tasks calling export already know that they have to request resolution of these jars to get them.

To keep compatibility with the existing export file we'll just need to re-dump all of this information in a new field and deprecate out the 'libraries' field (maybe in 1.1.0 remove this field).  This will cause some bloat in the short term but shouldn't cause any backwards incompatibility issues.

issue_body:
As discussed in #1022, the built-in help for all pants options that take an argument is wrong. Example:

```
[drift pants (master)]$ ./pants -h |grep -A 2 logdir
-d <dir>, --logdir <dir>
                        Write logs to files under this directory. (default:

Specifying `-d/foo/bar/baz` or `-d=/foo/bar/baz` work, specifying `-d /foo/bar/baz` does not. Examining the code, it seems like pants relies on the argparse defaults for printing the help. Unfortunately, pants behavior has diverged from the argparse defaults.

issue_title: RuntimeError when ffmpeg filter reduces # of frames
issue_body:
System: Linux, ffmpeg 3.3.6, imageio 2.3.0

This is a follow-up to Issue #294. That user was trying to pull I-frames from a video file, and ffmpeg.py needed some fiddling to put the ffmpeg parameters in the right order. That was solved in Pull #321.

I'm trying to do the exact same thing -- actually found that issue page using google. But although the parameters now get passed in ok, a new error soon crops up. To reproduce:

```python
import imageio
output_params = ['-vf', "select='eq(pict_type\,I)'"]
path = '/path/to/video.mp4'
reader = imageio.get_reader(path, format='FFMPEG', print_info=False, output_params=output_params)
print('num frames:', len(reader))
for i, img in enumerate(reader):
print(i)
```

For, say, a video w/ 100 frames and only 1 I-frame, the output is:

```
num frames: 100
0
```

... and then the exception:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/e/.local/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py", line 565, in _read_frame_data
    (len(s), framesize))
RuntimeError: Frame is 0 bytes, but expected 230400.

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/e/.local/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/imageio/core/format.py", line 397, in iter_data
    im, meta = self._get_data(i)
  File "/home/e/.local/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py", line 396, in _get_data
    result, is_new = self._read_frame()
  File "/home/e/.local/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py", line 585, in _read_frame
    s, is_new = self._read_frame_data()
  File "/home/e/.local/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py", line 571, in _read_frame_data
    raise CannotReadFrameError(fmt % (self._pos, err1, err2))
imageio.core.format.CannotReadFrameError: Could not read frame 0:
Frame is 0 bytes, but expected 230400.
```

The problem is that ffmpeg reports the total number of frames in the video, and imageio then expects to read that number of frames. But the pipe from ffmpeg contains fewer frames, based on the filter parameters that were used. imageio then chokes when it iterates past the last frame returned, expecting more.

Best option might be to just stop iterating silently, maybe print a warning. Don't think there's a clever way to get the "real" number of returned frames. ffmpeg's just passing raw video using the image2pipe "muxer", so there are no headers in the data w/ frame numbers, timestamps, etc. You can get frame info out of band, using e.g.

```
ffprobe -select_streams v -show_frames -show_entries frame=pkt_pts_time,pict_type -of csv=print_section=0 /home/e/video\ dupes/test_v.flv
```

But that's slow and requires parsing the video file again.

cc @rtibbles

I used the pyscenic command line interface (CLI) and it is a bit limited in that it won't return a simple list of pruned regulons with their targets. I believe this is important for interpreting the AUC matrix of activity scores and knowing what the targets are of the transcription factors. I cobbled together some python code to get this list for downstream analysis (see below). If you could make this standard output from the `pyscenic ctx' output that would be helpful. The current 'CTX' pruned file has a non-standard means of parsing and appears to have a lot of information that is of secondary interest. 

``` python
import pandas as pd
import os
from pyscenic.transform import df2regulons as df2regs
import json

# use function from pyscenic for parsing the 'pyscenic ctx' output
def _df2regulons(fname):
  ext = os.path.splitext(fname,)[1]
  df = pd.read_csv(fname, sep=',', index_col=[0,1], header=[0,1], skipinitialspace=True)
  df[('Enrichment', 'Context')] = df[('Enrichment', 'Context')].apply(lambda s: eval(s))
  df[('Enrichment', 'TargetGenes')] = df[('Enrichment', 'TargetGenes')].apply(lambda s: eval(s))
  return df2regs(df)

# Form dictionary of { TF : Target } pairs from 'pyscenic ctx' output.
rdict = {}
regulons = _df2regulons('joint_ctx.csv')
for reg in regulons:
  targets = [ target for target in reg.gene2weight ]
  rdict[reg.name] = targets

# Write to JSON for import to R
rjson = json.dumps(rdict)
f = open("regulons.json","w")
f.write(rjson)
f.close()
```

Then export into R with : 

```r
library(jsonlite)
regs <- read_json('regulons.json', simplify = T)
```

issue_title: Create test cases for network module

issue_body:
The method _'_wrapped_request()'_ of the ProfitBricksService class has the attribute _'json'_, which is used in calling the _request()_ method of a requests Session object.

But this argument of the method _request()_ was introduced with version _2.4.3_ of requests, earlier versions of requests did not support this attribute. But e.g. version 2.2.1 of requests is widely used, for instance in Ubuntu 14.04 LTS.

The method _'_wrapped_request()'_ is a (more or less) private method of the ProfitBricksService class, and the parameter _'json'_ of this method is used nowhere inside the ProfitBricksService class. That's why I would propose to remove this argument to stay compatible with Python requests 2.2.1.

issue_title: Could not authenticate: Did not find necessary cookies.
issue_body:
Downloading class: learn/learning-how-to-learn
Starting new HTTPS connection (1): class.coursera.org
Starting new HTTPS connection (1): www.coursera.org
Logged in on coursera.org.
Starting new HTTPS connection (1): class.coursera.org
Could not authenticate: Did not find necessary cookies.

There are several courses I've tried to download that fail.  I switched to including the cookies option (-c) and that helped for 2 courses, but several others remain.

The documentation states to use the option "--on-demand" for on demand courses.  When you use that option, it states that "--on-demand" has been deprecated.  There are two on-demand courses that the url looks incorrect when coursera-dl tries to access them.  

For the courser intro to public speaking, the URL is:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/public-speaking/home/welcome

And this is what coursera-dl tries to access:
https://class.coursera.org/public-speaking/lecture

It's trying to access an on-demand course with a not on-demand URL and gives a 404 error.  But there is no way to tell it that it's an on-demand course.

I have the same issue with the course what-is-social:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/what-is-social/home/welcome

issue_body:
Once #718 is merged, consider our API:

### Issue 1
```python
dmm_session = nidmm.Session('Dev1')
dc_session = nidcpower.Session('Dev2')
scope_session = niscope.Session('Dev3')

# channel attributes are accessed by indexing the channels container.
l = dc_session.channels[0].current_level

# device attributes are accessed directly on the session.
c = dc_session.channel_count

# But this is equivalent. Does it matter?
c = dc_session.channels[''].channel_count
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 2
Another potentially confusing problem is that all attribute are accessed on the `Session` but also by indexing the `channels` container:

```python
# Sets range on all channels
scope_session.range = 5.0
# Set range only on channel 1
scope_session.channels[1].range = 2.0
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 3
Functions that are channel based are similar to attributes. But those that aren't (i.e. `commit()`) are only available on the `Session`, not the `channels` container., except that functions that are _not
```python
scope_session.fetch(...) # OK
scope_session.channels[0]fetch(...) # OK
scope_session.commit() # OK
scope_session.channels[0].commit() # Raises!
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 4

Some suggested to follow nidaqmx-python precedent and have an "all_channels" member.

```python
# Sets range on all channels
scope_session.all_channels.range = 5.0
```
But then what about `nidmm` which has one channel (some would say no channels) and most of the C/LV API doesn't even have channel inputs? This is hardly ideal:
```python
dmm_session.all_channels.range = 10.0 # What channels?
```

issue_title: processHandshake is unnecessarilly strict in UTF-8 decoding
issue_body:
We see cases where some browsers, in foreign locale's trigger the default strict decoding of utf-8 during processHandshake. It seems like lenient utf-8 decoding should be done.

The error:
```
encodings.utf_8 in decode
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xe9 in position 647: invalid continuation byte



Stacktrace (most recent call last):

  File "autopush/websocket.py", line 85, in wrapper
    return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "autopush/websocket.py", line 313, in processHandshake
    return self.parent_class.processHandshake(self)
  File "autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 2761, in processHandshake
    (self.http_status_line, self.http_headers, http_headers_cnt) = parseHttpHeader(self.http_request_data)
  File "autobahn/websocket/protocol.py", line 388, in parseHttpHeader
    raw = data.decode('utf8').splitlines()
  File "encodings/utf_8.py", line 16, in decode
    return codecs.utf_8_decode(input, errors, True)
```

Couldn't this use 'ignore' on the decode line to ignore anything it can't coerce?

issue_title: Short (<1 day) durations not supported well
issue_body:
`isodate` will return `datetime.timedelta` objects for periods shorter than 1 day, instead of an `isodate.Duration` instance.
However, `cast_duration` only checks for the latter and not the former, which results in exceptions when trying to re-cast an already casted value.

issue_title: Behavior of `R_lib` and `lib_path` under local config `CONF`
issue_body: Currently `DSC::R_lib` only checks & installs R libraries without loading them automatically, and `DSC::lib_path` only provide search path for scripts to source without tracking changes. This design makes sense when they are global to all modules. When under module specific `@CONF`, however, we may want to additionally auto-load R library and keep track of file changes under `lib_path`.

issue_title: Controller is missing migration for empty/false Dockerfile values
issue_body:
Last night I have upgraded from `1.0.0` to `1.1.0` (ended up doing it via migration).

I've backed up the `db_wal` and `registry` from `1.0.0` and restored it to `1.1.0` as it per guidelines.

I have a couple of apps with Procfile's like these:
*app1*) `web: unicorn -p $PORT`
or
*app2*) `web: nginx -p . -c nginx.conf`

When I got to spawning releases from registry
*app1* launched but remained inaccessible because PORT was never expanded to 5000 (no wonders why).
*app2* failed to launch and as it looks like some args specified in the Procfile were omitted when appending to docker run command.

I'm suspecting this has something to do with this commit (and a couple of commits prior to this one): https://github.com/deis/deis/commit/d8b83435dfddd1a95a57178c8654a61357309d61

Using env vars in Procfile seems to be a valid practice providing extra flexibility for app packaging.
Am I missing something here? (I could be in fact, since I haven't found any other report of this)

issue_title: store: Run daemon as privileged, enabling faster PARALLEL write mode
issue_body:
The amazing @aledbf shared this with me:

```
5:56:09 AM <aledbf> check this commit https://github.com/ceph/ceph-docker/commit/92393e1c9129d49b33cf1bb1896690e07dc01458
6:09:25 AM <aledbf> I change deis-store-daemon adding --privileged and this is the result
6:09:28 AM <aledbf> before:
6:09:28 AM <aledbf> filestore(/var/lib/ceph/osd/ceph-2) mount: enabling WRITEAHEAD journal mode: checkpoint is not enabled
6:09:28 AM <aledbf> after:
6:09:28 AM <aledbf> filestore(/var/lib/ceph/osd/ceph-2) mount: enabling PARALLEL journal mode: fs, checkpoint is enabled
6:09:34 AM <aledbf> :)
```

The commit he referenced is https://github.com/ceph/ceph-docker/commit/92393e1c9129d49b33cf1bb1896690e07dc01458 - it looks like we've unintentionally been running the daemons in a slower mode! Running the container as privileged enables PARALLEL mode instead of the slower WRITEAHEAD, which is the default.

We should explore this. Ideally we can figure out the explicit privilege that we can [cap-add](https://docs.docker.com/reference/run/#runtime-privilege-linux-capabilities-and-lxc-configuration) when we run the container.

However, this isn't a straightforward change - @aledbf reports that it isn't possible to convert data from WRITEAHEAD to PARALLEL, and that he didn't notice an increase in write speed:
```
2:14:27 PM <aledbf> yeah, but is not possible to migrate from WRITEAHEAD :(
2:16:36 PM <aledbf> I don't see improvement in the speed but yes in the amount of simultaneous operations maintaining the same speed
```

At the very least, we should investigate the differences in these write modes to see if we can gain some performance here. We often see "blocked requests" in Ceph health even when an appropriate number of daemons is up, so perhaps this has to due with parallel writes?

issue_title: Make shell completion supported, change recommended sourcing method
issue_body:
cc @ranantha, I'd like us to make the current completion support an officially supported part of the CLI for GlobusWorld. I think that would be a nice time to add it in; it's a bit of a showy feature which has been done for a while and therefore requires basically no additional work.


The code has been sitting almost entirely untouched (and working) for over a year.
I've been using the current shell completion code for a while now and haven't had any issues with it. It works really well, especially in ZSH where the completion support is a bit superior.


A lot of tools use a nice trick for getting their shell completion stuff into people's bashrc:
`mytool --completion` prints the completer, and users are told to `eval "$(mytool --completion)"`

I like this. Could we add it, maybe move shell completion into the packaged CLI source dir?
We could then document this as a full feature of the CLI.

One thing that we'd need to add, I think, is some way to determine which shell to use. I think `bash` is a sane default, so:
`globus --completion` can do bash completion, and
`globus --completion zsh` can do `zsh` completion


@corpulentcoffee, @jaswilli, I know you guys have been less keen on shell completion than I. Does the change I'm suggesting (putting the current completion scripts into a hidden `--completion` option and documenting it) sound acceptable?

For a timeseries with an unknown data generating model, the *autocorrelation function (ACF)* and [*partial autocorrelation function (PACF)*](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partial_autocorrelation_function) help in identifying the order of an Autoregressive `ARMA(p, q)` model. The [Box-Jenkins method  uses ACF and PACF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Box%E2%80%93Jenkins_method#Autocorrelation_and_partial_autocorrelation_plots) for this purpose. Depending on the shape of the ACF and PACF, one can derive a model for fitting the data.

The difference between ACF and PACF:

- The ACF calculates the linear relationship between the values at timestep `t` and `t-k` (if one looks at the autocorrelation of lag `k`). So this is an univariate regression problem, which can be solved by Ordinary Least Squares (OLS).
- The PACF calculates the linear relationship between the values at timestep `t` **up to** `t-k` (if one looks at the partial autocorrelation of lag `k`). So this is a **multivariate** regression problem, which can be solved by Ordinary Least Squares (OLS), too. See this [post at SO](https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/18599/pacf-manual-calculation) for clarification.

#### Current Status

Afaics,`tsfresh` already provides the functions

- [`mean_autocorrelation`](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/feature_calculators.py#L264)
- [`autocorrelation`](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/feature_calculators.py#L1158)

If I derive it correctly from the statement `"autocorrelation": [{"lag": lag} for lag in range(10)],` from the [`feature_extraction.settings`](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/settings.py#L108), the latter is done for the lags 0 (which is trivially 1) to 9.

`tsfresh` also fits an AR process by the method [`ar_coefficient`](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/feature_calculators.py#L945). I need to confess that I don't get which params are calculated. It says in the [feature_extraction settings](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/settings.py#L117)
```python
"ar_coefficient": [{"coeff": coeff, "k": k} for coeff in range(5) for k in [10]],
```
So `coeff` stands for the number of coefficients, i.e. fitting an `AR(coeff)` model, but what does `k` stand for? Could one decide to fit several AR-processes, whose parameter settings could be identified by the ID `k`? Am I getting this right?
If so, then `tsfresh` fits an arbitrary defined AR-model.

#### Include PACF values
Including the PACF values, e.g. by using the [`statsmodels.tsa.stattools.pacf`](http://www.statsmodels.org/dev/generated/statsmodels.tsa.stattools.pacf.html#statsmodels.tsa.stattools.pacf), would reveal some imformation that can be exploited by a subsequent classifier. I recommend using the `method='ols'` parameter. Here is a small example:

- The AR(3) model:
```python
ar_process = [1] + [3.5] + [8.25] + 47 * [0]  # 50 data points, first 3 calculated by hand
for i in range(3, len(ar_process)):
    ar_process[i] = \
    2.5 * ar_process[i - 1] + \
    -1.5 * ar_process[i - 2] + \
    0.5 * ar_process[i - 3] + \
    1
print(ar_process[:10])
# > [1, 3.5, 8.25, 16.875, 32.5625, 61.21875, 113.640625, 209.5546875, 385.03515625, 706.076171875]
```
- PACF and ACF with 49 lags
```python
lags = len(ar_process)-1
f, axarr = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(20,5))
plot_pacf(ar_process, method='ols', lags=lags, ax=axarr[0].axes)
plot_acf(ar_process, lags=lags, ax=axarr[1].axes)
plt.suptitle('lags: {}'.format(lags))
plt.show()
```
![pacf_acf_lags_49](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18031651/27803095-16e734ac-6028-11e7-8e2b-c052a522c340.png)
- PACF and ACF with 10 lags

```python
lags = 10
f, axarr = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(20,5))
plot_pacf(ar_process, method='ols', lags=lags, ax=axarr[0].axes)
plot_acf(ar_process, lags=lags, ax=axarr[1].axes)
plt.suptitle('lags: {}'.format(lags))
plt.show()
```
![pacf_acf_lags_10](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18031651/27803112-2ce0f932-6028-11e7-8cd5-1ff0c7bb7476.png)

- PACF: `method='ols'` vs `method='ywu'`

```python
lags = 10
f, axarr = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(20,5))
plot_pacf(ar_process, method='ols', lags=lags, ax=axarr[0].axes)
plot_pacf(ar_process, method='ywunbiased', lags=lags, ax=axarr[1].axes)
plt.suptitle('ols vs ywunbiased')
plt.show()
```
![pacf_ols_vs_yw](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18031651/27803114-33a32ec0-6028-11e7-9be8-b711a337141f.png)


The method on the right side uses the [Yule-Walker equations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autoregressive_model#Yule.E2.80.93Walker_equations), which produces fundamentally different results than the `ols` approach and does not estimate the coefficients of the "normal" AR(p) formulation, at least in this example.

Hi, our company is trying to apply tsfresh on our credit scoring models for time series data but one problems came up.  During calculating index_mass, np.argmax(mass_centralized >= config["q"])+1) is trying to get the index of a time series for a single sample when the cumsum of this feature is greater than given quantile. however, the results is not a 'relative' index within a time series data for a single sample but relative to the index of the whole sample. 
For example, in the show case robot_execution_failures, extracted feature 'a__index_mass_quantile__q_0.1' increases when sample id increases. In my experience, using a pure random id to classify samples always has good statistical evaluation results but the models themselves are meaningless. 
We are thinking that is it a better way to reset dataframe index while calculating index mass? So that  the np.argmax will get the the index regardless of which part of the whole sample is the sample from  but just the position of the time in the series.

Suppose I have package that can be on Python 2 or Python 3, but it requires some backport modules for Python 2. If I start on Python 2, the upgrade to Python 3 gets blocked by the Python 2 dependencies. However, we all know they are not really blockers and we can try to remove them and workaround them. How effective this will be depends on how locked in your stack is to them. Here is a simple example with `jsonschema`. The recipe comes from `conda-forge` so we can clearly see `functools32` is a Python 2.7 dependency only from the [recipe](https://github.com/conda-forge/jsonschema-feedstock/blob/fba25c91b4b79579ba261e68667aaec78fa9f33d/recipe/meta.yaml#L23). issue_title: under Anaconda Distribution 4.2 - cannot create environments or make them appear in Notebook menus

issue_title: [Bug] conda removes folders from working directory when they share name with environment
issue_body:
conda removes folders from working directory when they share their name with the environment that you want to delete. You can find a minimal reproduction at the tagged commit linked here:

https://github.com/Korijn/condabug/tree/conda-env-remove
# Steps to reproduce

Run the following command from the root of the repository:

``` batch
> conda env create
> conda remove -n hw --all -y
```

_Conda has now deleted not only the `hw` environment but also the `hw` source folder!_

Since `conda env remove`'s behavior was redirect to the same code in a fairly recent release, you can also reproduce the bug like this:

``` batch
> conda env create
> conda env remove -n hw -y
```

You can even cause the behavior after the environment has already been deleted.

issue_title: 'R' (upper case) and 'r' (lower case) launches different versions of R language
issue_body:
Typing upper case and lower case R/r launches different versions of R programming language.
```
$ R --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.2 (2016-10-31) -- "Sincere Pumpkin Patch"
```
and
```
$ r --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.1 (2016-06-21) -- "Bug in Your Hair"
```
This happened after fresh install of miniconda3. Only default channels are used, no extra channels added.
```
$ conda list '^r' --show-channel-url
# packages in environment at /Users/username/miniconda3:
#
r                         3.3.2                  r3.3.2_0    defaults
r-base                    3.3.2                         1    defaults
(output trimmed for clarity)
```

I have two installations of R:
```
$ which -a {R,r}
/Users/username/miniconda3/bin/R
/usr/local/bin/R
/Users/username/miniconda3/bin/r
/usr/local/bin/r
```
When executing R/r from their original sources, the versions are correct:
```
$ /Users/username/miniconda3/bin/R --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.2 (2016-10-31) -- "Sincere Pumpkin Patch"
$ /Users/username/miniconda3/bin/r --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.2 (2016-10-31) -- "Sincere Pumpkin Patch"
$ /usr/local/bin/r --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.1 (2016-06-21) -- "Bug in Your Hair"
$ /usr/local/bin/R --version | head -n1
R version 3.3.1 (2016-06-21) -- "Bug in Your Hair"
```
My understanding is that my `$PATH` is being searched from left to right:
```
$ echo $PATH
/Users/username/miniconda3/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:/opt/X11/bin:/usr/local/munki
```

I'm on Mac:
```
$ uname -v
Darwin Kernel Version 15.6.0: Fri Feb 17 10:21:18 PST 2017; root:xnu-3248.60.11.4.1~1/RELEASE_X86_64
```

Any help in trying to understand why this happens would be appreciated.

I have been attempting use build features for the packaging of the cling c++ interpreter. Indeed `cling` depends on a patched version of `clang` and `llvm`.

Before I get into build features, the Dependency schema is

```
LLVM [cling]  <------ Clang [cling] <--------- Cling
```

Also, at runtime, Cling depends on `libcxx`, the clang c runtime.

**First approach with features**

Following the approach with the `Debug` empty package in the documentation, I created an empty conda package called `cling-patches` which tracks the `cling`  feature.

```
package:
  name: cling-patches
  version: 1

build:
  number: 0
  track_features:
    - cling
```

Then, I built two variants of Clang, one of which declares a `cling` features and depends on `cling-patches` while the other variant does not. 

My initial understanding was that installing `LLVM`, `Clang` and `cling-patches` together would result in have the cling variants of everything, while it did not.

In fact, the even while depending on `cling-patches`, the default variant of the LLVM package is always chosen! Looking into how others used build features, I came across the statement

https://github.com/conda-forge/blas-feedstock/blob/master/recipe/meta.yaml#L19

```
    # The "blas" package must always track a feature, else it will be
    # prioritised over all others.
```

I ended up having rely on this behavior to create the first dependency chain.

The current approach is that llvm and clang track features too (llvmcling for llvm and clangcling for clang). But as soon as `libcxx` is installed, the version of llvmdev and clang are downgraded.

**Prerequistes:**
- Your organization must be registered in Submission Portal before you can submit your variant data
- Variant-level not case-level
- Single variants or a set of variants (such as compound heterozygotes)

issue_title: Fetch with callback object does not work in pysam v0.8.3
issue_body:
Hello,

I am using a callback object with fetch() in order to process individual reference sequences:

    class Counter:
        mCounts = 0

    def __call__(self, alignment):
        self.mCounts += 1

    bamfile = pysam.Samfile('test.bam', 'rb')
    for seqId, seqLen in zip(bamfile.references, bamfile.lengths):
        c = Counter()
        bamfile.fetch(seqId, callback = c )
        print c.mCounts

For pysam v0.7.7 this code counts the number of reads mapped to each reference sequence. Under v0.8.3, the callback is not called. Are callback objects no longer supported or is this a bug?

It would appear to be a bug with callbacks in general, since even a simple callback function does not work:

    def my_fetch_callback( alignment ):
        print str(alignment)

    bamfile = pysam.Samfile('test.bam', 'rb')
    for seqId, seqLen in zip(bamfile.references, bamfile.lengths):
        bamfile.fetch(seqId, callback = my_fetch_callback )

issue_title: az login token expiring in 45 minutes to a hour when use with terraform
issue_body:
Running Azure Cli on a MAC and --version is (2.0.29)

Using Azure Cli for authentication with Terraform.  Once we login we are automatically logged out of cli between 45 minutes to a hour.  Documentation shows that the token should last for up to 14 days - https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/authenticate-azure-cli?view=azure-cli-latest

Is there anyway to see what the token expiration is set to after executing az login?  I tried to use --debug but it didn't show anything useful.  What is the best way to debug this issue?

Thank you

issue_title: Error when creating VM with Plan Information

We are trying to recreate a Cloudera Linux VM from the Cloud Shell cli.
There was a plan associated with the original VM, but whatever we supply the plan or not we are getting contradictory error messages;

issue_title: Unable to login with the new experience

I run `az login`, the browser is being opened, I select my account and see the success message: ``` You have logged into Microsoft Azure! You can close this window, or we will redirect you to the Azure CLI documents in 10 seconds. ``` However in the terminal I see an error: ``` ❯ az login Note, we have launched a browser for you to login. For old experience with device code, use "az login --use-device-code" [3186:3186:0702/202752.046404:ERROR:sandbox_linux.cc(378)] InitializeSandbox() called with multiple threads in process gpu-process. Created new window in existing browser session. Get Token request returned http error: 401 and server response: {"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"AADSTS70002: Error validating credentials. Luckily the old experience is still present, and when I use `az login --use-device-code`, I am able to successfully log in. **Environment summary** Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows) OS: Latest Arch Linux Shell: zsh Install method: [AUR/python-azure-cli](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/python-azure-cli/) with [AUR/python-azure-git](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/python-azure-git)
The output of az --version ``` ❯ az --version azure-cli (2.0.40) acr (2.0.28) acs (2.1.2) advisor (0.5.1) ams (0.1.1) appservice (0.1.36) backup (1.1.2) batch (3.2.4) batchai (0.3.1) billing (0.1.8) cdn (0.0.14) cloud (2.0.15) cognitiveservices (0.1.13) command-modules-nspkg (2.0.1) configure (2.0.17) consumption (0.3.1) container (0.2.0) core (2.0.40) cosmosdb (0.1.21) dla (0.1.0) dls (0.0.22) dms (0.0.1) eventgrid (0.1.12) eventhubs (0.1.3) extension (0.1.0) feedback (2.1.3) find (0.2.11) interactive (0.3.24) iot (0.1.21) keyvault (2.0.23) lab (0.0.22) maps (0.2.0) monitor (0.1.8) network (2.1.5) nspkg (3.0.2) policyinsights (0.1.0) profile (2.0.27) rdbms (0.2.5) redis (0.2.14) reservations (0.2.1) resource (2.0.32) role (2.0.26) servicebus (0.1.2) servicefabric (0.0.12) sql (2.0.27) storage (2.0.35) vm (2.0.35) Python location '/usr/bin/python' Extensions directory '/home/maximbaz/.azure/cliextensions' Python (Linux) 3.6.5 (default, May 11 2018, 04:00:52) [GCC 8.1.0] Legal docs and information: aka.ms/AzureCliLegal ```

I really don't like the style of "just link to some storage bucket that includes scripts that will download more scripts from another storage container and then all of this gets piped into a `sudo bash` session". It sets off all sorts of alarm bells.

Why can't there just be a page with all of the CI builds so I can choose what I want to install? I'd much rather just have a tarball that I can extract and run. And maybe a convenience link for those that really want to blindly throw binaries all over their system with no regard for their package manager.

I don't even get why there's any `sudo` involved at all. It's completely unneeded on OS X and shouldn't be necessary on Linux either, where `az` could easily be installed in a local user directory.

issue_title: az login with service principal and cert don't work on mac
issue_body:
The same cert that works on PC won't work on mac. Below is the script I used to download pfx file from keyvault and convert to private key to be used to login.

# 1. create certificate in keyvault and use it for service principal
```bash
$defaultPolicyFile = "$credentialFolder\default_policy.json"
az keyvault certificate get-default-policy -o json | Out-File $defaultPolicyFile -Encoding utf8 
az keyvault certificate create -n $CertName --vault-name $vaultName -p @$defaultPolicyFile
az ad sp create-for-rbac -n $spnName --role contributor --keyvault $vaultName --cert $certName 
```
# 2. download certificate 
```bash
az keyvault secret download --vault-name $VaultName -n $CertName -e base64 -f $pfxCertFile
```
# 3. convert pfx to private key
```bash
openssl pkcs12 -in $pfxCertFile -clcerts -nodes -out $keyCertFile -passin pass:
```
# 4. login
```bash
az login --service-principal -u "http://$spnName" -p $keyCertFile --tenant $tenantId
```
Here is the error I get on mac (it works on PC):
Get Token request returned http error: 401 and server response: {"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"AADSTS70002: Error validating credentials. AADSTS50012: Invalid client secret is provided.\r\nTrace ID: 180c2517-075c-441b-804d-f53f60824f00\r\nCorrelation ID: e12a59d9-e40a-48a9-8a11-7399be1080d5\r\nTimestamp: 2018-08-18 06:12:32Z","error_codes":[70002,50012],"timestamp":"2018-08-18 06:12:32Z","trace_id":"180c2517-075c-441b-804d-f53f60824f00","correlation_id":"e12a59d9-e40a-48a9-8a11-7399be1080d5"}

issue_title: az group deployment fails when property contains "="
issue_body:
### Description

When I run a deployment with an equals sign in a parameter, I get a failure.  This is particularly impactful when sending an sasToken or base64 encoded string in as a parameter value.

It appears the problem was introduced by https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/commit/3753b503aa8f9d21a28ca8fbeccd5d1623eafdda.  The function `_try_parse_key_value_object` splits on '=' prior to parsing as json.  I suspect this order needs to be reversed.

The following reduction test can be used to duplicate the problem.

    $ cat /tmp/azure.json
    {
        "$schema":        "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2015-01-01/deploymentTemplate.json#",
        "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
        "parameters": {
            "testParameter": {
                "type": "string"
            }
        },
        "resources": [
            {
                "comments":   "Public IP Address",
                "type":       "Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses",
                "name":       "Test",
                "apiVersion": "2016-03-30",
                "location":   "[resourceGroup().location]"
            }
        ]
    }

    $ cat /tmp/azure-params.json
    {
        "testParameter": "ac=d"
    }

    $ az group deployment validate --verbose --resource-group <RESOURCE GROUP> --template-file /tmp/azure.json --parameters @/tmp/azure-params.json
    Please provide a value for 'testParameter' (? for help): sdf
    {
      "error": {
        "code": "InvalidTemplate",
        "details": null,
        "message": "Deployment template validation failed: 'The template parameters '{\n\"testParameter\": \"ac' in the parameters file are not valid; they are not present in the original template and can therefore not be provided at deployment time. The only supported parameters for this template are 'testParameter'. Please see https://aka.ms/arm-deploy/#parameter-file for usage details.'.",
